title: carex parryana subsp. idahoa
intro: Carex parryana subsp. idahoa, also known as Idaho sedge, is a perennial sedge species native to the western United States. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it beneficial in treating various health issues. The plant's health benefits include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Carex parryana subsp. idahoa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with its roots often used in traditional medicine to create a remedy for various ailments.
